summary: Full backend + frontend testing of the Lead Management module. Backend: 17/17 pytest tests pass covering CRUD, search, status/propertyType filters, pagination math, role-based access (ADMIN vs AGENT), agent visibility scoping, and admin-only assign/delete. Frontend: 13/13 UI flow steps pass — sidebar nav, list rendering, search debounce, validation, add/edit modals, detail page, notes editor, delete confirm, status filter. No bugs found in the Lead module.
critical_code_review_comments: lead.service.ts (getLeads): assignedAgentId query param is applied AFTER the AGENT role override, so an AGENT cannot accidentally widen scope — good. However, if an ADMIN passes ?assignedAgentId=<x>, it correctly filters by that agent. OK.

users.routes.ts (GET /api/users): returns ALL users including ADMINs and is accessible to ANY authenticated user (including AGENTs). For a multi-tenant or larger system this could leak admin emails; consider restricting to ADMIN role or returning only AGENTs since the only consumer is the lead-assignment dropdown.
lead.controller.ts (editLead/removeLead/assignLead): use `e: any` for Prisma error matching. Tighten to `e: unknown` with proper type-guard for P2025 to satisfy strict TS.

lead.controller.ts (editLead): non-admin AGENTs can currently PUT any lead by id (no ownership check). If business intent is that AGENTs can only edit their own assigned leads, the controller/service should enforce that. Right now any authenticated user can update any lead. Worth confirming with product.
Auth/register endpoint normalizes emails to lowercase silently — fine, but document it (took one test rerun to spot).
test_credentials: admin@realestate.com / Admin@2036 (ADMIN). Test agents auto-registered via /api/auth/register (TEST_agent_<rand>@example.com / AgentPass@123).
seed_data_creation: Tests create their own leads prefixed 'TEST_' and clean them up in a session-scoped fixture. Test agent users remain in DB (TEST_agent_*@example.com) — harmless, but main agent may want a cleanup script.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Lead Management module is green end-to-end. Backend test file at /app/backend/tests/test_leads.py is reusable as regression — run with: REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=<url> pytest backend/tests/test_leads.py -v. Note: /api/users is currently open to any authenticated user (returns admins too); flag if security tightening is requested. Also note: AGENT role can currently PUT/edit ANY lead — only DELETE and ASSIGN are admin-gated. Confirm with product if edit should be ownership-scoped.
rca_of_issue: N/A — no bugs found in this iteration.
